title: Chrome Mirage, Vol. 1
tonic_progression: Em (Aeolian, ignition on the cracked hardpan, the engine catching cold under a bleached white sky) -> G (Mixolydian, the relative major opening up as the wheels bite and the salt flat unrolls flat to the horizon) -> D (Mixolydian, motorik cruise locking in, the chrome car settled into its run) -> Bm (Dorian, the heat-shimmer warping the horizon, the first mirage rippling the air) -> A (Mixolydian, the throttle opens, dust pluming, the run gathering real speed) -> E (Mixolydian, the supercharger whine, full forward drive across the open flat) -> F#m (Dorian, FULL-THROTTLE PEAK — the car wide open across the cracked salt, the album's fastest and most relentless) -> D (Mixolydian, the heat-mirage bridge, the horizon dissolving into liquid silver, momentum held but the air going strange) -> Bm (Aeolian, the rusted refinery towers rising out of the shimmer, iron and decay passing on the right) -> A (Dorian, the long straight after, the flat going on forever, the run easing) -> G (Mixolydian, the white sky beginning to bruise gold, the heat breaking as the sun finally tips down) -> Em (Aeolian, the engine ticking cool on the salt as the sun drops, home to the ignition tonic, the dust settling)
narrative_arc: Twelve tracks carry the listener across a salt-flat wasteland at high noon, from a cold ignition on cracked desert hardpan to the long cool-down as the sun finally drops behind the rusted refinery towers. This is a DRIVING album — dusty desert-rock-meets-synthwave, gated tom grooves, distorted slide-guitar-style saw leads, motorik forward drive, sun-scorched and arid rather than neon-wet — built for the coder who wants forward momentum without franticness, the engine pulling steady across an endless white-hot flat. Tracks 1-3 are ignition and the lock-in: the supercharged chrome muscle car catching cold under a bleached white sky (1, E minor, the engine turning over, gated toms entering one at a time like cylinders firing), the wheels biting and the salt flat unrolling flat to the horizon (2, G Mixolydian, the relative major opening up, the run beginning), and the motorik cruise settling into its groove, the chrome body shimmering against the cracked hardpan (3, D Mixolydian, the krautrock-steady pulse locked in). Tracks 4-7 are the run gathering and breaking into full throttle: the heat-shimmer warping the horizon, the first mirage rippling the air ahead (4, B minor Dorian, the HEAT-SHIMMER MOTIF entering, a wavering detuned figure that bends the pitch like air over hot salt); the throttle opening, dust pluming behind the car, real speed gathering (5, A Mixolydian); the supercharger whine rising, full forward drive across the open flat (6, E Mixolydian); and the FULL-THROTTLE PEAK — the car wide open across the cracked salt, the album's fastest and most relentless, the distorted slide-guitar saw lead screaming over a hammering gated-tom groove (7, F# minor Dorian, BPM ceiling 118). Tracks 8-10 are the heat-mirage bridge and the refinery passage: the horizon dissolving into liquid silver, momentum held but the air going strange and warped (8, D Mixolydian, the heat-mirage bridge, the HEAT-SHIMMER MOTIF at its most warped and liquid); the rusted refinery towers rising out of the shimmer, iron and decay passing on the right, a darker grinding groove (9, B minor Aeolian, the REFINERY-DRONE MOTIF, a low rusted distorted drone like wind through dead pipework); and the long straight after, the flat going on forever, the run easing back into open cruise (10, A Dorian). Tracks 11-12 are the cool-down: the white sky finally beginning to bruise gold at the edges, the murderous heat breaking as the sun tips down (11, G Mixolydian, the THROTTLE-RIFF easing off, the groove relaxing); and the engine ticking cool on the salt as the sun drops behind the towers, home to the ignition tonic, the dust settling and the day's run over (12, E minor, returning to the opener's key, the HEAT-SHIMMER MOTIF stilling as the air finally cools, a satisfied resolved close). A listener riding this album end to end should feel a single sun-scorched run across an endless white flat — cold ignition, building speed, full-throttle apex, the strange warped heat of the mirage, the rust of the refinery, and the cool drop of the sun — arid and dusty and driving the whole way, never neon-wet, never frantic, the chrome car and the salt and the heat the only things in the world.

recurring_motifs.motif: THROTTLE-RIFF (album primary motif) — a driving, palm-muted-feeling distorted saw riff built on the root-flat7-root-fifth motorik shape, the sonic rendering of the supercharged engine pulling forward: a gritty, slightly overdriven analog saw doubled an octave down on a fuzz-bass layer, locked to the gated-tom motorik pulse, dusty and dry (short room, no lush wet reverb) so it feels sun-scorched and arid rather than neon-wet. This is the engine of the album — the figure that pulls the listener forward across the flat. It is NOT a saga motif; it is local to Chrome Mirage.
recurring_motifs.transformation: Track 2 introduces the THROTTLE-RIFF at a steady cruising drive as the wheels bite and the run begins (recognizable, locked-in, motorik). Track 5 brings it back HARDER AND MORE OVERDRIVEN as the throttle opens and dust plumes, the riff pushed into more distortion as speed gathers. Track 7 (full-throttle peak) plays it at its MOST RELENTLESS AND SCREAMING — the riff hammered wide open at full distortion over the fastest gated-tom groove, the album's most aggressive statement. Track 11 EASES it OFF — the same riff de-tuned-down and pulled back into a relaxed lope as the heat breaks and the sun tips down, the engine still pulling but no longer straining. Track 12 plays it ONE LAST TIME COOL AND SETTLED — back at the ignition tonic, the riff ticking down to a satisfied close as the engine cools on the salt, the run over.

recurring_motifs.motif: HEAT-SHIMMER MOTIF (album secondary motif) — a wavering, slowly-detuning lead figure that bends the pitch like air rippling over hot salt: a thin glassy saw or sine lead run through a slow pitch-vibrato / tape-warble and a touch of chorus so the pitch visibly WAVERS and SMEARS, the sonic rendering of the heat-shimmer warping the horizon and the mirage rippling the air. Dusty and high in the mix, dry rather than wet (the shimmer is hot dry air, not water). This is the album's atmosphere figure — the thing that makes Chrome Mirage feel sun-scorched and warped rather than cool.
recurring_motifs.transformation: Track 4 introduces the HEAT-SHIMMER MOTIF wavering faintly on the horizon as the first mirage ripples the air ahead — a thin warped figure high in the mix, the pitch just beginning to bend. Track 7 (full-throttle peak) plays it AT SPEED AND DISTRESSED — the shimmer figure stretched and stressed across the screaming peak, the air over the salt boiling as the car tears through it. Track 8 (heat-mirage bridge) brings it to its MOST WARPED AND LIQUID — the whole horizon dissolving into liquid silver, the motif at its most detuned and smeared, the air gone fully strange. Track 12 plays it STILLING AND COOLING — the waver slowing and settling as the heat finally breaks and the air cools at sundown, the shimmer dying away to nothing as the run ends.

recurring_motifs.motif: REFINERY-DRONE MOTIF (album tertiary / textural figure) — a low, rusted, distorted drone like wind moaning through dead pipework and corroded refinery towers: a detuned low saw/noise layer with a slow filter sweep and a metallic resonant grind, dry and industrial and arid, the sonic rendering of the rusted refinery towers in the distance and the iron-and-decay of the wasteland. It is what gives Chrome Mirage its rust-and-iron weight beneath the speed — the dead industry standing in the heat.
recurring_motifs.transformation: Track 1 plants the REFINERY-DRONE faintly in the distance during the cold ignition — a low rusted drone on the horizon under the bleached sky, the dead towers standing far off as the engine catches. Track 9 brings it FULL AND GRINDING as the rusted refinery towers rise out of the shimmer and pass on the right — the drone foregrounded, metallic and corroded, wind through the dead pipework, the album's darkest and most industrial texture. Track 12 returns it FAINT AND DISTANT ONE LAST TIME as the engine cools and the sun drops behind the towers — the rusted drone receding on the horizon as the run ends, bookending the album's opening.

tracks.title: Cold Start on the Hardpan

sections.name: intro
sections.instrumentation: dry dusty pad in E minor under a bleached-white-sky shimmer, a faint distant REFINERY-DRONE on the horizon (low rusted distorted drone, the dead towers standing far off), a single low fuzz-bass note holding, no drums yet — the engine not yet caught, the heat-haze stillness of high noon on the cracked salt

sections.name: verse
sections.instrumentation: gated toms enter ONE AT A TIME like cylinders firing cold (kick on 1, a single gated tom hit, then a second), dry analog bass settling onto the motorik root, the refinery-drone holding faint beneath, a thin dusty arpeggio flickering like heat-haze

sections.name: chorus
sections.instrumentation: + the full gated-tom groove locking in for the first time (motorik kick + gated toms + dry snare on 3), a clean-then-slightly-overdriven saw lead tracing a wide arid melody, dry short-room reverb only (sun-scorched not wet), the engine fully caught and the salt flat opening ahead

sections.name: bridge
sections.instrumentation: drums thin to just the gated-tom pulse, the refinery-drone surfaces faint on the horizon, the saw lead holds a long bent note like heat warping the air, the held moment before the run begins

sections.name: outro
sections.instrumentation: the gated-tom groove drives forward, fuzz-bass pulling motorik, the harmony leaning toward G major (the relative major) as the wheels begin to bite and the run opens up next track

tracks.musicgen_prompt: synthwave 98 BPM E minor, dry dusty analog pad under a bleached-white-sky shimmer, gated tom groove entering one tom at a time like cylinders firing cold, motorik kick and dry snare, dry overdriven analog saw lead tracing a wide arid melody, dusty fuzz bass pulling forward, a faint low rusted distorted drone on the distant horizon like wind through dead refinery towers, a thin flickering heat-haze arpeggio, short dry room reverb only no lush wet reverb, sun-scorched and arid and motorik, the cold ignition of a supercharged chrome muscle car on cracked desert hardpan at high noon, desert-rock-meets-synthwave, instrumental, no vocals
tracks.cover_prompt: synthwave 1985 album cover, sun-bleached desert salt-flat palette of cracked white-grey hardpan under a blinding bleached-white sky, a lone supercharged chrome muscle car parked on cracked desert salt at high noon, heat-shimmer rising faintly off the hardpan, distant rusted refinery towers as small dark silhouettes on the far horizon, harsh overhead noon light glinting off the chrome bodywork, no neon, no ocean, no palm trees, arid anticipatory ignition mood, painterly retro-1980s art, no text, 1024x1024
tracks.composer_notes: OPENER. Establishes the album's home tonic E minor and the full sonic palette: dry, dusty, sun-scorched gated-tom motorik groove (NOT neon-wet — keep reverb short and dry, this is hot dry desert air not a wet night city). The gated toms enter ONE AT A TIME like cylinders firing cold — the ignition rendered audible. The REFINERY-DRONE is planted faint and distant here (the dead towers on the horizon), to be paid off full and grinding in track 9 and faint again at the close in track 12. Keep it arid and anticipatory: the run hasn't really begun, the THROTTLE-RIFF proper enters next track. Do NOT let MusicGen add lush wet reverb, neon-synth gloss, ocean/coastal textures, or vocals — this is dusty desert-rock-meets-synthwave, dry and hot. Outro leans toward G major (the relative major) — the wheels begin to bite and the run opens up next track.

tracks.title: Salt Flat Run

sections.name: intro
sections.instrumentation: the gated-tom motorik groove already running in G Mixolydian (flat-7, F natural, giving the major a dusty rock color), dry fuzz bass locked to the pulse, dry pad opening wide like the salt flat unrolling to the horizon

sections.name: verse
sections.instrumentation: + the THROTTLE-RIFF entering for the first time at a steady cruising drive (gritty overdriven saw on the root-flat7-root-fifth motorik shape, doubled an octave down on fuzz bass), gated toms steady, dry snare on 3, the wheels biting and the run beginning

sections.name: chorus
sections.instrumentation: + a wide arid saw lead riding over the throttle-riff, dry short-room reverb, the salt flat unrolled flat and endless ahead, the chrome car settling into its forward pull

sections.name: verse2
sections.instrumentation: lead drops, the throttle-riff foregrounded, a dusty hi-hat-and-gated-tom texture driving, fuzz bass motorik beneath

sections.name: chorus2
sections.instrumentation: lead returns doubled an octave up on a thin dusty saw, full driving mix, the run wide open and steady

sections.name: outro
sections.instrumentation: the throttle-riff drives on, harmony settling toward D (priming the next track's D-major cruise), gated-tom groove unbroken, the forward pull continuing

tracks.musicgen_prompt: synthwave 104 BPM G major Mixolydian (flat seventh, F natural giving a dusty rock color), driving motorik gated tom groove, a gritty overdriven distorted saw throttle-riff on a root-flat7-root-fifth motorik shape doubled an octave down on fuzz bass, a wide arid saw lead riding over the top, dusty fuzz bass locked to the pulse, dry snare and gated toms, short dry room reverb only no lush wet reverb, sun-scorched and open and motorik, the wheels biting and a supercharged chrome muscle car settling into a fast run across an endless white salt flat at high noon, desert-rock-meets-synthwave, instrumental, no vocals
tracks.cover_prompt: synthwave 1985 album cover, sun-bleached desert salt-flat palette of cracked white-grey hardpan under a blinding bleached-white sky, a lone supercharged chrome muscle car tearing across an endless flat white salt plain at high noon seen from a low rear three-quarter angle, a thin dust trail kicked up behind the wheels, the flat unrolling dead-flat to a distant heat-shimmer horizon, harsh overhead noon light, no neon, no ocean, no palm trees, driving open released mood, painterly retro-1980s art, no text, 1024x1024
tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Relative major of E minor — the lit, open release of the run beginning. Track 1's E-minor ignition leans up into its relative G major as the wheels bite, the dusty Mixolydian flat-7 (F natural) keeping the major gritty and rock-leaning rather than sweet. This is the album's first turn from the held ignition into forward motion.
tracks.tempo_relationship_to_prior: +6 BPM. The motorik pulse firms and the THROTTLE-RIFF locks in for the first time — the run properly begins. Atlantis-style small increments are out; this is a DRIVING album, so the steps are bigger and the floor is higher (98 not 84).
tracks.composer_notes: FIRST APPEARANCE of the THROTTLE-RIFF (album primary motif), introduced at a steady cruising drive — the engine of the album, the figure that pulls the listener forward. The relative-major lift (E minor -> G major) opens the held ignition into forward motion. G Mixolydian (flat-7, F natural) keeps the major DUSTY and ROCK-LEANING, not sweet pop-major — this is desert-rock-meets-synthwave. Keep it dry and arid (short-room reverb only); keep the gated-tom motorik groove relentless and steady (krautrock-steady, not busy). Outro settles toward D — priming track 3's D-major motorik cruise (a step up the run toward speed).

tracks.title: Motorik Mile

sections.name: intro
sections.instrumentation: the motorik gated-tom groove locked dead-steady in D Mixolydian (flat-7, C natural), dry fuzz bass pulsing the krautrock eighth-note drive, dry pad shimmering with heat-haze

sections.name: verse
sections.instrumentation: + a hypnotic repeating dusty arpeggio (the motorik insistence, a single figure cycling), the throttle-riff implied beneath in the fuzz bass, gated toms steady, dry snare on 3, the chrome car settled fully into its run

sections.name: chorus
sections.instrumentation: + a wide arid saw lead on a long-breathed melody over the locked groove, dry short reverb, the cracked hardpan blurring past, the hypnotic forward pull at full lock

sections.name: breakdown
sections.instrumentation: drums thin to the bare motorik pulse, the dusty arpeggio cycling alone, the hypnotic heat-haze stretch of the open run

sections.name: chorus2
sections.instrumentation: full groove returns, lead doubled an octave down on a fuzzier saw, the motorik drive at its most hypnotic and locked

sections.name: outro
sections.instrumentation: the motorik groove drives on, a faint pitch-bend creeping into the lead as the harmony leans toward B minor — the first heat-shimmer about to warp the air next track

tracks.musicgen_prompt: synthwave 110 BPM D major Mixolydian (flat seventh, C natural), dead-steady krautrock motorik gated tom groove, hypnotic repeating dusty arpeggio cycling, a wide arid saw lead on a long-breathed melody, dusty fuzz bass pulsing eighth-note motorik drive, dry snare and gated toms locked and relentless, short dry room reverb only no lush wet reverb, sun-scorched and hypnotic and locked-in, a supercharged chrome muscle car settled fully into a steady motorik run across a blurring white salt flat at high noon, desert-rock-meets-synthwave, instrumental, no vocals
tracks.cover_prompt: synthwave 1985 album cover, sun-bleached desert salt-flat palette of cracked white-grey hardpan under a blinding bleached-white sky, a lone supercharged chrome muscle car at speed seen from directly behind low to the ground, the cracked salt rushing past in motion-blur streaks toward a flat distant heat-shimmer horizon, a steady dust trail, harsh overhead noon light glinting off chrome, no neon, no ocean, no palm trees, hypnotic motorik locked-in driving mood, painterly retro-1980s art, no text, 1024x1024
tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Up a perfect fifth from G major to D major (a forward, opening fifth-motion up the cycle — the run climbing toward speed, not sinking). Both Mixolydian, keeping the dusty rock flat-7 color consistent across the cruise. The fifth-up motion gives a sense of GAINING ground and momentum rather than the descending-fourths sinking of a darker album.
tracks.tempo_relationship_to_prior: +6 BPM. The cruise locks fully into its motorik groove at 110 — the hypnotic dead-steady krautrock pulse that defines the album's forward drive. Still climbing toward the peak; the steps stay big because the album drives.
tracks.composer_notes: The motorik lock-in — the most purely hypnotic, krautrock-steady track of the early run (think NEU!/Kraftwerk forward-pulse crossed with synthwave). The forward FIFTH-UP key motion (G -> D) gives a sense of gaining ground (deliberately the opposite of a sinking descending-fourths album). Keep the groove dead-steady and the arpeggio insistently cycling — the hypnosis of the open run IS the composition here. Dry and arid throughout. The outro lets a faint pitch-bend creep into the lead — foreshadowing the HEAT-SHIMMER MOTIF that enters next track. Outro leans toward B minor (the relative minor of D) — the first heat-shimmer about to warp the air.

tracks.title: Heat Shimmer

sections.name: intro
sections.instrumentation: pad in B minor Dorian (raised 6th, G natural, a dusty open color), the HEAT-SHIMMER MOTIF entering faintly high in the mix (a thin glassy saw with slow pitch-vibrato and tape-warble, the pitch wavering and smearing like air over hot salt), gated-tom groove easing slightly, dry fuzz bass

sections.name: verse
sections.instrumentation: + the heat-shimmer figure wavering on the horizon, motorik groove steady but a touch looser, a dusty arpeggio rippling, the throttle-riff implied beneath, the first mirage rippling the air ahead

sections.name: chorus
sections.instrumentation: + an arid saw lead over the shimmer, the Dorian G natural keeping the minor open and warm-dusty rather than dark, dry short reverb, the horizon ahead beginning to ripple and bend in the heat

sections.name: bridge
sections.instrumentation: drums thin, the HEAT-SHIMMER MOTIF foregrounded alone wavering and smearing, the pad shimmering with heat-haze, the mirage at its first full ripple

sections.name: chorus2
sections.instrumentation: groove returns, lead and heat-shimmer figure intertwined, the warped air and the forward drive together

sections.name: outro
sections.instrumentation: the groove firms back up, harmony leaning toward A (priming the next track's A-major throttle-open), the heat-shimmer figure holding faint as the throttle is about to open

tracks.musicgen_prompt: synthwave 108 BPM B minor Dorian (raised sixth, G natural, dusty open color), motorik gated tom groove eased slightly looser, a thin glassy wavering lead with slow pitch-vibrato and tape-warble smearing the pitch like air rippling over hot salt, a rippling dusty arpeggio, an arid saw lead, dusty fuzz bass, dry snare and gated toms, short dry room reverb only no lush wet reverb, sun-scorched and warped and shimmering, the first heat-mirage rippling and bending the horizon ahead of a fast chrome muscle car on a white salt flat at high noon, desert-rock-meets-synthwave, instrumental, no vocals
tracks.cover_prompt: synthwave 1985 album cover, sun-bleached desert salt-flat palette of cracked white-grey hardpan under a blinding bleached-white sky, the horizon ahead warping and rippling into a wavering heat-mirage of liquid silver and distorted light, a lone supercharged chrome muscle car driving toward the rippling mirage, the cracked salt flat shimmering with rising heat-haze, harsh overhead noon light, no neon, no ocean, no palm trees, warped shimmering mirage mood, painterly retro-1980s art, no text, 1024x1024
tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Relative minor of D major — the run's first turn toward the strange and warped as the heat-mirage enters. The Dorian raised-6th (G natural) keeps this minor OPEN and warm-dusty rather than dark or mournful — the mirage is hallucinatory and shimmering, not threatening (the darkness comes later, at the refinery in track 9). A momentary cooling-into-strangeness, not a drop in energy.
tracks.tempo_relationship_to_prior: -2 BPM. The album's only dip in the climbing first half — the groove eases SLIGHTLY looser as the air goes warped and strange, a brief held breath in the heat-mirage before the throttle opens again in track 5. The dip is small and deliberate; the forward drive never stops.
tracks.composer_notes: BRIDGE / FIRST APPEARANCE of the HEAT-SHIMMER MOTIF (album secondary / atmosphere motif), entering faintly on the horizon — a thin glassy wavering lead with slow pitch-vibrato and tape-warble that bends and smears the pitch like air over hot salt (the thing that makes Chrome Mirage feel sun-scorched and warped rather than cool; keep it DRY — the shimmer is hot dry air, not water). B Dorian (raised 6th, G natural) keeps the minor open and warm-dusty, NOT dark — the mirage is hallucinatory, not threatening. This is the album's one BPM dip in the climb (a held breath in the heat) before the throttle opens in track 5; keep the motorik drive present underneath even as it eases. Outro leans toward A (priming track 5's A-major throttle-open). Do not let MusicGen darken this into a minor-key dirge or add wet reverb — it's a shimmering warped DRY heat-haze over a still-driving groove.

tracks.title: Throttle Open

sections.name: intro
sections.instrumentation: the motorik gated-tom groove firming back up hard in A Mixolydian (flat-7, G natural), dry fuzz bass driving, dry pad, the throttle about to open

sections.name: verse
sections.instrumentation: + the THROTTLE-RIFF back HARDER AND MORE OVERDRIVEN (the gritty saw pushed into more distortion as speed gathers), gated toms hammering steadier and harder, dry snare on 3, dust pluming behind the car

sections.name: chorus
sections.instrumentation: + a muscular arid saw lead riding the harder throttle-riff, dry short reverb, the throttle wide open and real speed gathering across the flat

sections.name: verse2
sections.instrumentation: lead drops, the harder throttle-riff foregrounded and grinding, the gated-tom groove driving relentless, fuzz bass overdriven

sections.name: chorus2
sections.instrumentation: lead returns doubled, the full muscular driving mix, the run accelerating toward the peak

sections.name: outro
sections.instrumentation: the throttle-riff drives on at full grit, harmony leaning toward E (priming the next track's E-major supercharger drive), the acceleration unbroken

tracks.musicgen_prompt: synthwave 114 BPM A major Mixolydian (flat seventh, G natural), hard driving motorik gated tom groove, a gritty heavily overdriven distorted saw throttle-riff doubled on fuzz bass pushed into more distortion, a muscular arid saw lead, dusty overdriven fuzz bass, dry snare and hammering gated toms, short dry room reverb only no lush wet reverb, sun-scorched and accelerating and muscular, the throttle opening wide and dust pluming behind a supercharged chrome muscle car gathering real speed across a white salt flat at high noon, desert-rock-meets-synthwave, instrumental, no vocals
tracks.cover_prompt: synthwave 1985 album cover, sun-bleached desert salt-flat palette of cracked white-grey hardpan under a blinding bleached-white sky, a lone supercharged chrome muscle car at full acceleration kicking up a huge billowing dust plume behind it across a white salt flat, the chrome bodywork blurred with speed, harsh overhead noon light glinting hard off the metal, the flat stretching to a heat-shimmer horizon, no neon, no ocean, no palm trees, accelerating muscular driving mood, painterly retro-1980s art, no text, 1024x1024
tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Up a perfect fifth from D major to A major (the forward, climbing fifth-motion resumes after the heat-shimmer's relative-minor detour — the run accelerating back up the cycle toward speed). Mixolydian flat-7 (G natural) holds the dusty rock color. The fifth-up gain-of-ground motion drives the acceleration.
tracks.tempo_relationship_to_prior: +6 BPM. The throttle opens and the climb toward the peak resumes hard after track 4's brief heat-mirage dip — the biggest single jump back into the acceleration, the run gathering real speed.
tracks.composer_notes: RETURN of the THROTTLE-RIFF, now HARDER AND MORE OVERDRIVEN than its track-2 introduction — the riff pushed into more distortion as speed gathers and dust plumes. The forward fifth-up key motion (D -> A) resumes the acceleration after track 4's heat-shimmer detour. This is the muscular, gathering-speed track that sets up the full-throttle peak in track 7. Keep it dry and arid (no wet reverb) but let the distortion and grit build — the engine is straining now. Hammering gated toms, harder than the earlier cruise. Outro leans toward E (priming track 6's E-major supercharger drive — the climb to the peak continues).

tracks.title: Supercharger

sections.name: intro
sections.instrumentation: the hard motorik gated-tom groove driving in E Mixolydian (flat-7, D natural), a rising supercharger-whine swell (a slow pitch-rising filtered saw, like the blower spooling up), dry fuzz bass driving, dry pad

sections.name: verse
sections.instrumentation: + a hard grinding rhythm-saw chugging beneath, gated toms hammering, the supercharger-whine cresting and falling, the full forward drive across the open flat, dry snare on 3

sections.name: pre-chorus
sections.instrumentation: build: the supercharger-whine rising again, dry tom-fill rolls, the groove tightening, the run cresting toward the peak

sections.name: chorus
sections.instrumentation: a screaming muscular saw lead on a wide-open phrase over the hard motorik groove, dry short reverb, the supercharger at full whine, the car wide open and the peak in sight

sections.name: break
sections.instrumentation: drums to the bare motorik pulse, the supercharger-whine swelling alone, the grinding rhythm-saw chugging, the held tension before full throttle

sections.name: chorus2
sections.instrumentation: full hard return, lead doubled an octave down on a fuzz saw, the most relentless drive yet, the run cresting straight into the peak

sections.name: outro
sections.instrumentation: the groove drives on at full grit, a supercharger-whine cresting, harmony leaning toward F# minor as the run goes wide open into the full-throttle peak next track

tracks.musicgen_prompt: synthwave 116 BPM E major Mixolydian (flat seventh, D natural), hard relentless motorik gated tom groove, a rising supercharger-whine swell like a blower spooling up (slow pitch-rising filtered saw), a hard grinding chugging rhythm-saw, a screaming muscular wide-open saw lead, dusty overdriven fuzz bass, dry snare and hammering gated toms, short dry room reverb only no lush wet reverb, sun-scorched and screaming and relentless, the supercharger whining at full whine as a chrome muscle car drives wide open across a white salt flat cresting toward full throttle at high noon, desert-rock-meets-synthwave, instrumental, no vocals
tracks.cover_prompt: synthwave 1985 album cover, sun-bleached desert salt-flat palette of cracked white-grey hardpan under a blinding bleached-white sky, a tight low front-quarter view of a supercharged chrome muscle car bearing down at full speed, the exposed blower and chrome supercharger jutting through the hood catching harsh noon light, a long dust plume trailing, the white salt flat blurred with speed, no neon, no ocean, no palm trees, screaming relentless full-drive mood, painterly retro-1980s art, no text, 1024x1024
tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Up a perfect fifth from A major to E major (the forward, climbing fifth-motion continues toward the peak — the run at its hardest gain of ground). Mixolydian flat-7 (D natural) keeps the dusty rock color even at full drive. The relentless fifth-up climb is the harmonic engine of the acceleration into the peak.
tracks.tempo_relationship_to_prior: +2 BPM. The penultimate step before the peak — the supercharger whining the run up to 116, one notch below the 118 ceiling, the climb's final crest before full throttle.
tracks.composer_notes: BRIDGE INTO PEAK. The supercharger-whine swell (a slow pitch-rising filtered saw, the blower spooling up) is the signature texture here, cresting and falling as the run goes wide open. The relentless fifth-up climb (A -> E) is the run's hardest gain of ground, the crest straight into the full-throttle peak in track 7. Keep it hard, dry, and arid — grinding chugging rhythm-saw beneath a screaming lead, hammering gated toms, but NEVER wet or neon-glossy. This is the build; the peak proper is next track. Outro leans toward F# minor (the relative minor of the coming peak) — the run goes wide open into full throttle next track.

tracks.title: Wide Open

sections.name: intro
sections.instrumentation: the hammering motorik gated-tom groove at full speed in F# minor Dorian (raised 6th, D# natural, keeping the minor driving and open rather than dark), dry overdriven fuzz bass, the run already wide open

sections.instrumentation: + the THROTTLE-RIFF at its MOST RELENTLESS AND SCREAMING (hammered wide open at full distortion over the fastest gated-tom groove), the HEAT-SHIMMER MOTIF stretched and distressed high in the mix (the air over the salt boiling), dry snare cracking on 3

sections.name: chorus
sections.instrumentation: a distorted slide-guitar-style saw lead SCREAMING over the hammering groove (bending and wailing like a slide guitar at full throttle), dry short reverb, the car wide open across the cracked salt, the album's most aggressive and relentless statement

sections.name: verse2
sections.instrumentation: lead drops, the screaming throttle-riff and distressed heat-shimmer figure intertwined, the gated-tom groove hammering at full speed, fuzz bass at full grit

sections.name: outro

tracks.musicgen_prompt: synthwave 118 BPM F-sharp minor Dorian (raised sixth, D-sharp natural, driving and open), hammering motorik gated tom groove at full speed, a relentless screaming heavily distorted saw throttle-riff at maximum distortion, a distorted slide-guitar-style saw lead bending and wailing like a slide guitar at full throttle, a stretched distressed wavering heat-shimmer lead high in the mix like boiling air, dusty overdriven fuzz bass at full grit, dry cracking snare and hammering gated toms, short dry room reverb only no lush wet reverb, sun-scorched and full-throttle and relentless and screaming, a supercharged chrome muscle car wide open at maximum speed across cracked white salt at high noon, desert-rock-meets-synthwave, instrumental, no vocals
tracks.cover_prompt: synthwave 1985 album cover, sun-bleached desert salt-flat palette of cracked white-grey hardpan under a blinding bleached-white sky, a dramatic low side-on view of a supercharged chrome muscle car at absolute full speed tearing across a white salt flat, a vast billowing dust plume exploding behind it, the chrome bodywork streaked with extreme motion-blur, the heat-shimmer horizon boiling, harsh overhead noon light blazing off the metal, no neon, no ocean, no palm trees, full-throttle relentless screaming peak mood, the album's most dramatic hero cover, painterly retro-1980s art, no text, 1024x1024
tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Relative minor of A major and a half-step up from track 6's E (the run's apex pitch — F# minor, the most driving and relentless key of the album). The Dorian raised-6th (D# natural) keeps even this peak minor DRIVING and OPEN rather than dark or tragic — Chrome Mirage's peak is full-throttle EXHILARATION, not catastrophe; the energy is speed and grit, the key stays muscular and open. (Note this is the relative minor of the prior track's V-of-E region, the climb resolving its tension into the peak's driving minor.)
tracks.tempo_relationship_to_prior: +2 BPM to the album's BPM ceiling of 118 — the full-throttle peak, the fastest and most relentless track. 118 is the validation ceiling and the absolute top of the run; the car is wide open here and nowhere else.
tracks.composer_notes: PEAK / the full-throttle apex (the longest track at 300s to give the wide-open run real room). The THROTTLE-RIFF is at its MOST RELENTLESS AND SCREAMING (hammered wide open at full distortion over the fastest gated-tom groove) and the HEAT-SHIMMER MOTIF returns AT SPEED AND DISTRESSED (stretched and stressed across the screaming peak, the air boiling). The signature lead here is the DISTORTED SLIDE-GUITAR-STYLE SAW LEAD screaming and bending like a slide guitar at full throttle — the album's defining sound at its most aggressive. F# Dorian (raised 6th, D# natural) keeps the peak DRIVING and OPEN, NOT dark or tragic — this peak is full-throttle EXHILARATION (the opposite of a catastrophe-apex album); the energy is speed and grit, not dread. Keep it dry and arid even at full grit (no wet reverb, no neon gloss). 118 BPM is the validation ceiling — the absolute top of the run. Outro eases toward D (priming track 8's D-major heat-mirage bridge) — the peak begins to give way to the strange warped air ahead.

tracks.title: Liquid Silver Horizon

tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Relative major of F# minor — the peak resolves into the open, dreamlike major of the heat-mirage bridge. Mixolydian flat-7 (C natural) holds the dusty color. tracks.composer_notes: BRIDGE / the heat-mirage at its most total. The HEAT-SHIMMER MOTIF is at its MOST WARPED AND LIQUID — the thin wavering lead detuned and smeared to its strangest, the whole horizon dissolving into liquid silver (keep it DRY — the silver is a mirage of hot dry air, NOT water; do not let MusicGen or the cover read as ocean/coastal). The relative-major lift off the peak (F# minor -> D major) opens the come-down into a dreamlike warped major. This is the album's most hallucinatory track — momentum held (112) but the world gone strange. Keep the motorik groove present underneath even as it loosens; keep it arid and dry. Outro leans toward B minor (the relative minor) as a darker shape rises out of the shimmer — the rusted refinery towers about to emerge next track.

tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Relative minor of D major (the same B-minor pitch as track 4, but now AEOLIAN/natural-minor where track 4 was Dorian — a deliberate darkening of the album's earlier shimmer key). Where track 4's B Dorian was open and shimmering (the hallucinatory mirage), track 9's B Aeolian is dark and grinding (the rust-and-iron reality of the dead refinery emerging out of that same shimmer) — the album's one genuinely dark turn, the flat-6 lending iron weight.
tracks.tempo_relationship_to_prior: -6 BPM. The wind-down continues as the run passes the refinery — the groove goes heavier and grinding rather than fast (the darkness is in the TEXTURE and the drone, not in speed). The back half is easing back toward cruise.
tracks.composer_notes: FULL APPEARANCE of the REFINERY-DRONE MOTIF (planted faint in track 1, paid off here) — full and grinding, a low rusted detuned distorted drone with metallic resonant grind, like wind through dead pipework, the rusted refinery towers rising out of the shimmer and passing on the right. This is the album's ONE genuinely dark turn (B AEOLIAN, deliberately darkening the B-Dorian shimmer key of track 4) — the iron-and-decay reality of the dead industry standing in the heat. The flat-6 of natural minor lends the iron weight. Keep the darkness in the TEXTURE (the grinding drone, the chugging rhythm-saw, the heavy gated toms) rather than in speed — it's a heavier grinding groove, not a faster one. Dry and arid still (no wet reverb). Outro leans toward A (priming track 10's A-Dorian long straight) as the towers pass behind and the run eases back into the open.

tracks.cover_prompt: synthwave 1985 album cover, sun-bleached desert salt-flat palette of cracked white-grey hardpan under a bleached-white sky just beginning to warm at the very edges, an endless dead-straight track receding to a vanishing point across a vast empty white salt flat, a lone chrome muscle car cruising the long straight, the rusted refinery towers now small and far behind, the flat stretching endlessly ahead, harsh noon light just starting to soften, no neon, no ocean, no palm trees, open easing endless cruise mood, painterly retro-1980s art, no text, 1024x1024
tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Down a step from B minor to A minor, and the mode OPENS back from Aeolian (the refinery's dark natural-minor) to Dorian (raised-6th, F# natural) — the minor going open and warm-dusty again after the grinding dark of track 9. The run leaves the rust behind and eases back into open cruise; the Dorian lift signals the easing without yet reaching the major-key cool-down (that's track 11).
tracks.tempo_relationship_to_prior: +4 BPM. A small lift back up as the run opens onto the long straight after the refinery — the groove relaxes back into an easy open cruise (faster than the grinding refinery but no longer the gathering-speed climb of the first half). The wind-down is gentle and undulating, not a straight drop.
tracks.composer_notes: The long straight after the refinery — the run easing back into open cruise. The mode OPENS from track 9's dark B Aeolian to A Dorian (raised 6th, F# natural) — the minor going open and warm-dusty again, leaving the rust behind. This is a relaxed open-cruise track (a small BPM lift back to 110 from the refinery's 106, but the energy is RELAXED, not gathering — the flat going on forever, the run easy). Keep it wide, arid, and open; keep the motorik groove steady but unhurried. Outro leans toward G (priming track 11's G-major cool-down) with a first faint gold creeping into the bleached sky — the heat beginning to break as the sun starts to tip down.

tracks.title: The Heat Breaks

sections.name: intro

sections.instrumentation: drums thin, the eased throttle-riff loping alone, the pad warming gold, the held relief as the sun drops toward the horizon

sections.name: chorus2

sections.name: outro

tracks.musicgen_prompt: synthwave 100 BPM G major Mixolydian (flat seventh, F natural), warming dry analog pad, a relaxed de-tuned-down distorted saw throttle-riff eased off into a loping groove, a warm arid saw lead on a relaxed melody, dusty fuzz bass, dry snare and easy loping gated toms, short dry room reverb only no lush wet reverb, sun-scorched and easing and warming and relieved, the murderous high-noon heat finally breaking as the sun tips down and the bleached sky bruises gold over a chrome muscle car winding down its run on a white salt flat, desert-rock-meets-synthwave, instrumental, no vocals
tracks.cover_prompt: synthwave 1985 album cover, desert salt-flat palette shifting from bleached-white toward warming amber-gold as the heat breaks, the white sky now bruising deep gold and amber at the horizon as the sun lowers, a lone chrome muscle car cruising relaxed across the cracked salt flat in the warming gold light, the heat-shimmer subsiding, long shadows beginning to stretch across the hardpan, no neon, no ocean, no palm trees (a desert sundown, not a coastal sunset), easing warming relieved winding-down mood, painterly retro-1980s art, no text, 1024x1024
tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Down a step from A minor to G major (the run's turn from minor cruise into the warming relief of the heat breaking). Mixolydian flat-7 (F natural) holds the dusty rock color even into the warming. This is the same G-major key as track 2 (the run's beginning) — deliberately bookending the start of the run with its winding-down, the relief of the heat breaking answering the release of the run beginning.
tracks.tempo_relationship_to_prior: -10 BPM. The largest single step down of the album — the run clearly winding down now as the heat breaks and the sun tips down, the groove relaxing out of cruise toward the close. The wind-down accelerates here as the day ends.
tracks.composer_notes: COMEDOWN / the heat breaks. The THROTTLE-RIFF returns EASED OFF — de-tuned down and pulled back into a relaxed lope, the engine still pulling but no longer straining, the run winding down. G major (Mixolydian, flat-7 F natural) deliberately bookends track 2's G major (the run's beginning) — the relief of the heat breaking answering the release of the run beginning, the same key opening and closing the journey's middle. The bleached white sky finally bruises gold as the sun tips down (this is a DESERT SUNDOWN, NOT a coastal sunset — keep the cover free of ocean/palms/beach per the danger-zone constraint). Keep it warm, relaxed, arid, dry — relieved, the worst of the day over. Outro leans toward E minor (the relative minor, home to the ignition tonic) as the sun nears the horizon and the run nears its end.

tracks.title: Engine Cooling at Sundown

sections.name: intro

sections.name: verse
sections.instrumentation: + a warm arid saw lead on a settled resolving melody, gated toms loping slow and easy, dry snare on 3, dusty fuzz bass relaxing, the engine ticking cool on the salt as the day's run ends, the dust beginning to settle

sections.name: chorus
sections.instrumentation: + the lead resolving warm over the settled groove, dry short reverb, the heat-shimmer stilling away to nothing as the air cools, the satisfied close of a long run across the flat

sections.name: bridge
sections.instrumentation: drums thin to the bare easing pulse, the cool throttle-riff and the stilling heat-shimmer figure resolving together, the faint refinery-drone receding, the sun dropping below the towers

sections.name: outro
sections.instrumentation: the groove eases to a stop, the pad sustains E minor home, the heat-shimmer figure dies away to nothing as the air finally cools, the faint refinery-drone fades on the horizon, the engine ticks cool and silent, the dust settles, a satisfied resolved close on the home tonic — the run over, the day done

tracks.musicgen_prompt: synthwave 94 BPM E minor, dry pad settling warm and home, a cool settled de-tuned distorted saw throttle-riff loping to a satisfied close, a warm arid saw lead on a resolving melody, a stilling slowing wavering heat-shimmer lead dying away as the air cools, a faint distant low rusted drone receding on the horizon, dusty fuzz bass relaxing, dry snare and slow easy gated toms easing to a stop, short dry room reverb only no lush wet reverb, sun-scorched and cooling and settled and satisfied, a supercharged chrome muscle car's engine ticking cool on the salt as the sun drops behind rusted refinery towers and the day's run ends, desert-rock-meets-synthwave, instrumental, no vocals
tracks.cover_prompt: synthwave 1985 album cover, desert salt-flat palette of deep amber-gold and burnt orange sky over cracked grey salt as the sun drops, the sun a low deep-orange disc dropping behind distant rusted refinery towers on the horizon, a lone supercharged chrome muscle car parked and still on the cracked salt flat, long blue-grey shadows stretching across the hardpan, the heat-haze gone, the chrome bodywork catching the last warm low light, no neon, no ocean, no palm trees (a desert sundown behind dead refinery towers, not a coastal sunset), resolved cooling settled satisfied mood, painterly retro-1980s art, no text, 1024x1024
tracks.key_relationship_to_prior: Relative minor of G major, returning HOME to the album's ignition tonic E minor (track 1's key) — the run resolves back to where it started, the engine cooling on the salt at the key it cold-started in. The relative-minor turn from track 11's warming G major into the settled E-minor home gives the album a satisfying full-circle close: the cold ignition and the cool sundown share a tonic, the day's run framed by E minor at both ends.
tracks.tempo_relationship_to_prior: -6 BPM to the album's floor of 94 (the slowest track) — the run winds fully down to a stop as the engine cools and the sun drops, the closer settling below the opener's 98 to mark the end of the day's drive. 94 is the album's lowest BPM (still well within the 88-118 driving band — this is a driving album, so even the close keeps a relaxed pulse rather than going fully ambient).
tracks.composer_notes: CLOSER / full-circle home. Returns HOME to the ignition tonic E minor (track 1's key) — the cold-start and the cool-down share a tonic, framing the day's run. This track resolves the album's three motifs all at once: the THROTTLE-RIFF plays ONE LAST TIME COOL AND SETTLED (ticking down to a satisfied close as the engine cools), the HEAT-SHIMMER MOTIF STILLS (the waver slowing and dying away to nothing as the air finally cools at sundown), and the REFINERY-DRONE returns FAINT AND DISTANT one last time (receding on the horizon as the sun drops behind the towers, bookending track 1's faint distant drone). The bleached white sky has finally given way to a deep amber-gold desert SUNDOWN (NOT a coastal sunset — sun dropping behind rusted refinery towers, no ocean/palms/beach, per the danger-zone constraint). Keep it warm, settled, arid, dry, and satisfied — a resolved CLOSE on the home tonic, the run over, the day done. This is a driving album, so even the close keeps a relaxed pulse (94 BPM) rather than going fully ambient/drumless. Do NOT let MusicGen brighten this into neon, add wet reverb, or fail to resolve — the satisfied settled home cadence IS the close.

album_notes.overall_form: Ignition -> Lock-In -> The Run Gathers -> Full-Throttle Peak -> Heat-Mirage Bridge -> The Refinery -> Cool-Down over 12 tracks: a single sun-scorched DAY-RUN across a salt-flat wasteland at high noon, framed by E minor at both ends (cold ignition in track 1, cool sundown in track 12) — a full-circle DRIVING arc rather than a sinking or arching one. Chrome Mirage is dusty desert-rock-meets-synthwave: gated tom motorik grooves, distorted slide-guitar-style saw leads, relentless forward drive, sun-scorched and arid and DRY (short-room reverb only, never the lush wet neon-night reverb of a coastal synthwave album). Movements: IGNITION & LOCK-IN (tracks 1-3): the supercharged chrome muscle car catching cold under a bleached white sky, gated toms entering one at a time like cylinders firing (1, E minor, slowest-but-one at 98, the only late-drum-entry track); the wheels biting and the salt flat unrolling as the THROTTLE-RIFF locks in (2, G Mixolydian, the relative-major release of the run beginning); and the hypnotic krautrock motorik cruise at full lock (3, D Mixolydian). THE RUN GATHERS (tracks 4-6): the heat-shimmer warping the horizon as the HEAT-SHIMMER MOTIF enters (4, B minor Dorian, the album's one BPM dip — a held breath in the heat); the throttle opening and dust pluming as the THROTTLE-RIFF returns harder (5, A Mixolydian); and the supercharger whining the run wide open (6, E Mixolydian, the crest into the peak). FULL-THROTTLE PEAK (track 7, F# minor Dorian): the car wide open across the cracked salt, the album's fastest (118, the BPM ceiling) and longest (300s) track, the distorted slide-guitar saw lead SCREAMING over a hammering gated-tom groove — full-throttle EXHILARATION, not catastrophe (the Dorian raised-6th keeps even the peak driving and open). HEAT-MIRAGE BRIDGE & THE REFINERY (tracks 8-9): the horizon dissolving into liquid silver with the HEAT-SHIMMER MOTIF at its most warped (8, D Mixolydian, the dreamlike come-off-the-peak); and the rusted refinery towers rising out of the shimmer with the REFINERY-DRONE MOTIF full and grinding (9, B minor Aeolian, the album's ONE genuinely dark turn). COOL-DOWN (tracks 10-12): the long straight after, the run easing back into open cruise (10, A Dorian); the murderous heat breaking as the bleached sky bruises gold and the THROTTLE-RIFF eases off (11, G Mixolydian, bookending track 2's run-beginning key); and the engine ticking cool on the salt as the sun drops behind the refinery towers, HOME to the E-minor ignition tonic with all three motifs resolving (12, E minor, the satisfied full-circle close). The E-minor ignition of track 1 and the E-minor sundown of track 12 frame the day's run; the full-throttle F#-minor peak at track 7 is its center.
album_notes.key_strategy: A FORWARD-DRIVING key plan organized around fifth-and-step motion and a major/minor mode-shift that tracks the run's energy and the desert light — deliberately NOT a sinking descending-fourths plan (this is a driving album that gains ground, not an elegy that sinks). The album is FRAMED by E minor (Aeolian) at both ends: the cold ignition tonic (track 1) and the cool-down home (track 12) share a tonic, giving a full-circle close. The first half CLIMBS via forward FIFTH-UP motion through dusty MIXOLYDIAN majors (the gain-of-ground engine of the acceleration), with the minor keys reserved for atmosphere: E minor (ignition) -> G major Mixolydian (relative major, the run begins) -> D major Mixolydian (up a 5th, motorik lock-in) -> B minor Dorian (relative minor, the heat-shimmer enters, the one dip) -> A major Mixolydian (up a 5th, throttle opens) -> E major Mixolydian (up a 5th, supercharger) -> F# minor Dorian (relative-minor-of-A apex, the full-throttle peak). The Mixolydian flat-7 across all the major keys is the album's signature DUSTY ROCK color (G/D/A/E/G major all Mixolydian — desert-rock, not sweet pop-major); the minor keys use DORIAN (raised-6th, open and warm-dusty) for the shimmering/driving moods and AEOLIAN (natural minor) only for the two genuinely-grounded moments (the E-minor ignition/close and the B-minor refinery's dark turn). The back half WINDS DOWN via gentler step-and-relative motion: F# minor (peak) -> D major (relative major, the dreamlike heat-mirage bridge) -> B minor Aeolian (relative minor, DARKENED from track 4's B Dorian — the same pitch re-cast dark for the refinery, the album's one dark turn) -> A minor Dorian (down a step, mode re-OPENS from the refinery's Aeolian, the long straight) -> G major Mixolydian (down a step, the heat breaks, bookending track 2's run-beginning key) -> E minor Aeolian (relative minor, HOME to the ignition tonic, the satisfied full-circle close). Two deliberate echoes structure it: B minor appears twice (track 4 Dorian/shimmering, track 9 Aeolian/dark — the same pitch, the hallucinatory mirage vs. the rust-and-iron reality that emerges from it), and G major appears twice (track 2 the run beginning, track 11 the heat breaking — the release and the relief framing the journey's middle). The home E minor opens and closes. No closed-then-reopened saga seam (standalone Vol. 1): track 1 opens clean (no pickup) and track 12 closes clean (a satisfied resolved home cadence, the engine cooling, the run over).
album_notes.tempo_strategy: A DRIVING album's mid/upper BPM band (94-118) — appropriately faster and more forward than a coastal or elegiac synthwave album, but still capped to keep the coder focused, not frantic. The shape is a CLIMB to a single full-throttle peak then an undulating wind-down, not a symmetric arch. IGNITION & LOCK-IN: 98 (the cold ignition, the only late-drum-entry track, second-slowest) -> 104 (the THROTTLE-RIFF locks in, the run begins) -> 110 (the motorik cruise at full lock). THE RUN GATHERS: 108 (a -2 dip at track 4, the album's one step DOWN in the climb — a held breath in the heat-shimmer before the throttle opens) -> 114 (throttle opens) -> 116 (supercharger crests). FULL-THROTTLE PEAK: 118 (track 7, the BPM ceiling and the validation maximum — the car wide open, the fastest track, reached exactly once). HEAT-MIRAGE BRIDGE & THE REFINERY: 112 (-6 off the peak, the dreamlike come-down) -> 106 (-6, the grinding refinery, heavier-not-faster, the album's slowest mid-run point). COOL-DOWN: 110 (+4, the long straight, an easy open-cruise lift back up — the wind-down undulates rather than dropping straight) -> 100 (-10, the largest step down, the heat breaks) -> 94 (the album's floor, the slowest track, the engine cooling at sundown). The band 94-118 sits notably higher and tighter than an elegiac album (this is a DRIVING record — the motorik forward pulse is the point), with the steps mostly +/-6 (bigger than an elegy's patient +/-2). The single -2 dip at track 4 and the +4 lift at track 10 keep the arc undulating and alive rather than a mechanical ramp. All BPMs are within the 88-118 driving band specified for this album and within the 80-118 validation range (album range 94-118).
album_notes.motif_strategy: Three ALBUM-LOCAL motifs (all saga_recurring:false — this is a standalone Vol. 1, no saga, no carried leitmotifs). (1) THROTTLE-RIFF (primary motif) — a driving palm-muted-feeling distorted saw riff on the root-flat7-root-fifth motorik shape doubled an octave down on fuzz bass, locked to the gated-tom motorik pulse, dusty and dry: the sonic rendering of the supercharged engine pulling forward, the engine of the album. Introduced at a steady cruising drive as the run begins (track 2); harder and more overdriven as the throttle opens and dust plumes (track 5); most relentless and screaming at the full-throttle peak (track 7); eased off into a relaxed lope as the heat breaks (track 11); and one last time cool and settled, ticking down to a satisfied close as the engine cools (track 12). Its arc IS the run's energy: lock-in -> harder -> screaming peak -> eased off -> cooled. (2) HEAT-SHIMMER MOTIF (secondary / atmosphere motif) — a thin glassy wavering lead run through slow pitch-vibrato and tape-warble so the pitch bends and smears like air rippling over hot salt, dry and high in the mix: the album's heat-warped atmosphere, the thing that makes Chrome Mirage feel sun-scorched and warped rather than cool. Introduced wavering faintly on the horizon as the first mirage ripples (track 4); at speed and distressed across the screaming peak as the air boils (track 7); most warped and liquid at the heat-mirage bridge as the horizon dissolves into liquid silver (track 8); and stilling and cooling, dying away to nothing as the air finally cools at sundown (track 12). (3) REFINERY-DRONE MOTIF (tertiary / textural figure) — a low rusted detuned distorted drone with a slow filter sweep and metallic resonant grind, like wind through dead pipework, dry and industrial: the rust-and-iron weight beneath the speed, the dead industry standing in the heat. Planted faint and distant during the cold ignition (track 1); full and grinding as the rusted refinery towers rise out of the shimmer and pass (track 9, the album's darkest texture); and faint and distant one last time as the engine cools and the sun drops behind the towers (track 12, bookending track 1). The closer (track 12) resolves all three motifs at once: the throttle-riff cools and settles, the heat-shimmer stills, and the refinery-drone recedes — the full-circle close. Keep ALL motifs DRY and arid (short-room reverb only) — none of them should read as wet/neon; the album is hot dry desert air, not a wet night city.
album_notes.tracklist_continuity: Each track ends on a pitch that primes the next track's tonic, mostly via forward fifth-and-relative motion (the harmonic engine of a driving album that GAINS ground, not a sinking one). Specifically: 1 ends leaning toward G major (the relative major of E minor — the wheels bite, the run opens). 2 ends settling toward D (priming track 3's D-major motorik cruise, a forward fifth up). 3 ends leaning toward B minor (the relative minor of D — the first heat-shimmer). 4 ends leaning toward A (priming track 5's A-major throttle-open, a forward fifth up). 5 ends leaning toward E (priming track 6's E-major supercharger, a forward fifth up). 6 ends leaning toward F# minor (the relative minor of A / the coming peak — the run goes wide open). 7 ends easing toward D (the relative major of F# minor, priming track 8's heat-mirage bridge). 8 ends leaning toward B minor (the relative minor of D — a darker shape rising, the refinery emerging). 9 ends leaning toward A (priming track 10's A-Dorian long straight, the run easing). 10 ends leaning toward G (priming track 11's G-major cool-down, the heat beginning to break). 11 ends leaning toward E minor (the relative minor, home to the ignition tonic — the sun nears the horizon). 12 ends on a SATISFIED RESOLVED E-minor home cadence (the engine ticking cool, the run over — a CLEAN close, not an open seam: this is a standalone Vol. 1 with no handoff to a sequel or saga). The listener won't consciously hear the fifth-up climb or the relative-minor turns — they will feel the run gaining ground across the first half, the wide-open exhilaration of the peak, the strange warped air of the heat-mirage, the rust of the refinery, and the satisfying full-circle settle of the engine cooling home in the key it cold-started in.
album_notes.visual_continuity_notes: Twelve covers narrate the single sun-scorched DAY-RUN across the salt-flat wasteland: the LONE SUPERCHARGED CHROME MUSCLE CAR is the constant subject across all twelve (parked and cold in track 1, at full speed and exploding dust at the peak in track 7, parked and still at sundown in track 12); the CRACKED WHITE-GREY SALT-FLAT HARDPAN and the BLEACHED-WHITE NOON SKY are the constant ground (the sky only warming to amber-gold in the final two cool-down covers as the sun drops); the RUSTED REFINERY TOWERS recur as the wasteland's landmark (faint on the horizon in track 1, full and corroded as the album's industrial subject in track 9, the sun dropping behind them in track 12); and the HEAT-SHIMMER recurs as the album's signature distortion (faint in track 1, rippling the horizon in track 4, dissolving the whole horizon into liquid silver in track 8, subsiding as the heat breaks in track 11, gone at the cool sundown in track 12). The palette is SUN-BLEACHED DESERT: cracked white-grey salt, blinding bleached-white sky, harsh overhead noon light glinting off chrome, rust orange-brown on the dead refinery iron, warming to deep amber-gold and burnt orange only in the final two covers as the sun drops behind the towers. CRITICAL per the danger-zone constraint (keys: sunset, miami_vice): this is a DESERT/salt-flat album and the covers must stay DISTINCT from the coastal Sunset Drive and Miami Vice albums — NO palm trees, NO ocean/water/beach, NO coastal-sunset imagery; the warm sky in tracks 11-12 is a DESERT SUNDOWN behind rusted refinery towers, NOT a coastal sunset over water (the track-8 liquid-silver horizon is explicitly a heat-MIRAGE of hot dry air, not a body of water — the cover prompt says so). Every cover_prompt ends with 'no text, 1024x1024'. The covers should read as one sun-scorched desert day-run: cold chrome at high noon, building dust, a wide-open dust-explosion peak, a liquid-silver mirage, the rust of the dead refinery, and the cool deep-gold drop of the sun behind the towers.
album_notes.execution_notes_for_pipeline: MusicGen / ACE-Step is the renderer of record (license risk accepted). Every musicgen_prompt is explicit about 'instrumental' and ends 'no vocals'; there are NO choir or vocal references anywhere (this is an instrumental desert-rock-synthwave album — guitars-as-saws, gated toms, fuzz bass, drones, no singing). Per-track durations sum to 2940 seconds (49.0 minutes) for the 12 — comfortably over the 45-minute album floor. Long track: 7 (300s, the full-throttle peak, the longest — the wide-open run needs room). All other tracks sit at 240s. All BPMs within the 88-118 driving band specified for this album and the 80-118 validation range (album range 94-118); all durations within 180-360s. CRITICAL SONIC DIRECTION for prompt tuning: this album is DRY AND ARID, NOT neon-wet — every prompt says 'short dry room reverb only no lush wet reverb' and 'sun-scorched'; if MusicGen drifts toward lush-reverb neon-night synthwave, pull it back toward dusty desert-rock (overdriven saws, gated toms, fuzz bass, dry mix). The distorted slide-guitar-style saw lead is the album's defining timbre (most prominent at the peak, track 7). WEATHER ROUTING — FLAG FOR HUMAN/PIPELINE per the per-album-weather and 'TWC cities must match album theme' rules: the theme is a generic American-Southwest salt-flat desert (no specific real-world Atlantic/Japan/Soviet/Arctic anchor), so the encoder's default US NWS weather region is APPROPRIATE here (unlike the Atlantic/Japan/Soviet albums that needed custom non-US regions) — BUT for thematic fit consider routing 'chrome-mirage' / 'salt-flat' / 'desert' to a SOUTHWEST-DESERT US city set (recommend Bonneville/Wendover UT, Las Vegas NV, Barstow CA, Yuma AZ — high-desert salt-flat-adjacent cities matching the bleached-noon-desert anchor) rather than the generic default 4 cities; this is optional/nice-to-have, not a blocker (US NWS coverage works for all four). If no custom routing is added, the default US weather region is acceptable by exception (the theme is US-domestic desert). Cover prompts are SDXL-ready (1024x1024); per the future-albums native-aspect-gen rule, generate covers at native aspect buckets (1024 square + 1344x768 + 768x1344) from scratch rather than outpainting. Per the sync-drop rule for future albums, target a 1-shot synchronized public flip at one anchor timestamp (use run-album --publish-at), NOT the per-track trickle. Mind the YouTube caps for a 12-track album: the ~10/day channel rolling-24h cap, the 6/day GCP per-project video.insert cap, and Pacific-midnight resets — span the upload across a reset or stagger (publish-staggered 6/day) and set the sync-drop anchor accordingly, per the Vol. 2/Vol. 3 and saga-album lessons. DANGER ZONE: danger_zone_keys are ['sunset', 'miami_vice'] — this is a DESERT/salt-flat album that must stay distinct from the coastal Sunset Drive and Miami Vice albums. (a) NO coastal imagery in any cover (no palm trees, no ocean/water/beach, no coastal sunset — the warm sky in tracks 11-12 is a desert sundown behind rusted refinery towers; the track-8 liquid-silver horizon is a heat-mirage of dry air, not water — every relevant cover_prompt explicitly says 'no neon, no ocean, no palm trees'). (b) ORIGINAL TITLES checked clear of the double-hit rule (named object/dialogue in a specific film AND a track title on that film's canonical soundtrack): the titles are 'Cold Start on the Hardpan', 'Salt Flat Run', 'Motorik Mile', 'Heat Shimmer', 'Throttle Open', 'Supercharger', 'Wide Open', 'Liquid Silver Horizon', 'Rust and Iron', 'The Long Straight', 'The Heat Breaks', 'Engine Cooling at Sundown' — all are generic DESCRIPTIVE phrases (engine/desert/driving vocabulary), none is a named object/character/dialogue from a specific car or desert film that is ALSO a track on that film's soundtrack album, and none echoes a 'Drive' (2011) cue title or a Miami Vice OST track title (deliberately avoided 'Nightcall', 'A Real Hero', 'Drive', 'Crockett's Theme', etc.). 'Motorik' is a public-domain musicology term (the Krautrock beat), not a film/brand reference.
